column1: Under the Legal Framework Order, what major power was given to the President of Pakistan?
column2: The power to dismiss the national and provincial assemblies.

column1: Which party led Zimbabwe's freedom struggle and ruled the country after independence?
column2: ZANU-PF

column1: Who was the long-time leader of Zimbabwe from independence until 2017?
column2: Robert Mugabe

column1: Feature of Democracy: A democratic government rules within limits set by _____ and _____.
column2: Constitutional law; citizens' rights

column1: What is the Greek origin of the word 'democracy'?
column2: Demokratia

column1: In the Greek word 'Demokratia', what does 'demos' mean?
column2: People

column1: In the Greek word 'Demokratia', what does 'kratia' mean?
column2: Rule

column1: Provide the simple, starting definition of democracy used in the text.
column2: Democracy is a form of government in which the rulers are elected by the people.

column1: Why is the simple definition of democracy considered inadequate on its own?
column2: It could misleadingly include every government that holds an election, even if they only pretend to be a democracy.

column1: Who led a military coup in Pakistan in October 1999?
column2: General Pervez Musharraf

column1: What designation did Pervez Musharraf first give himself after the 1999 coup?
column2: Chief Executive

column1: What was the result of the 2002 referendum in Pakistan for General Musharraf?
column2: He was granted a five-year extension as President.

column1: Why did democracy activists criticize the 2002 referendum in Pakistan?
column2: It was reportedly based on malpractices and fraud.

column1: Which 2002 order amended the Constitution of Pakistan?
column2: Legal Framework Order

column1: In Musharraf's Pakistan, which body supervised the work of the civilian cabinet?
column2: National Security Council

column1: Who dominated the National Security Council in Pakistan under Musharraf?
column2: Military officers

column1: Why was Pakistan under Musharraf not a democracy despite having elected representatives?
column2: The final decision-making power rested with non-elected military officials and General Musharraf.

column1: Feature of Democracy: In a democracy, where must the final decision-making power rest?
column2: With those elected by the people.

column1: What is the name of the Chinese parliament?
column2: National People's Congress (Quanguo Renmin Daibiao Dahui)

column1: In China, which body has the power to appoint the President of the country?
column2: The National People's Congress

column1: Which organization must approve a candidate before they can contest elections in China?
column2: The Chinese Communist Party

column1: Why are elections in China not considered democratic?
column2: They do not offer the people a real choice between political alternatives.

column1: Since its independence in 1930, how often does Mexico hold presidential elections?
column2: Every six years

column1: What was the dominant political party in Mexico that won every election until 2000?
column2: PRI (Institutional Revolutionary Party)

column1: In Mexico, how did the PRI party use government teachers for political ends?
column2: Teachers were used to force parents to vote for the PRI.

column1: In Mexico, what dirty trick did the PRI use to disrupt voting on election day?
column2: Shifting polling booths from one place to another at the last minute.

column1: Feature of Democracy: A democracy must be based on a _____ and _____ election.
column2: Free; fair

column1: In a truly democratic election, what chance should those currently in power have?
column2: A fair chance of losing.

column1: Until 2015, which country denied women the right to vote?
column2: Saudi Arabia

column1: In Estonia, citizenship rules make it difficult for which group to get the right to vote?
column2: The Russian minority

column1: How does the value of a vote differ in Fiji's electoral system?
column2: The vote of an indigenous Fiji has more value than that of an Indian-Fijian.

column1: Feature of Democracy: In a democracy, each adult citizen must have _____ vote and each vote must have _____ value.
column2: One; one

column1: How did Robert Mugabe's government treat the constitution over the years?
column2: It changed the constitution several times to increase the President's powers and reduce accountability.

column1: What happened to public protests and demonstrations in Mugabe's Zimbabwe?
column2: They were declared illegal.

column1: How did the Zimbabwean government under Mugabe treat the judiciary?
column2: It ignored court judgments that went against it and pressurized judges.

column1: The example of Zimbabwe proves that popular approval of rulers is necessary but not _____.
column2: Sufficient

column1: In a democracy, what must the state respect in the period before elections?
column2: Basic rights of the citizen (like freedom of thought and expression).

column1: Who must protect the basic rights of citizens in a democracy?
column2: An independent judiciary

column1: Why is it argued that democracy leads to 'instability'?
column2: Because leaders keep changing frequently.

column1: What is the argument against democracy regarding decision-making speed?
column2: The need to consult many people leads to significant delays.

column1: Why do some argue that democracy leads to bad decisions?
column2: Elected leaders do not always know the best interest of the people.

column1: What is the argument against democracy regarding corruption?
column2: Democracy is based on electoral competition, which can lead to corruption.

column1: Quote: Abraham Lincoln defined democracy as government of the _____, by the _____, and for the _____.
column2: People; people; people

column1: Why is a clear definition of democracy needed when many countries use the term?
column2: Because very different kinds of governments call themselves democracy for different purposes.

column1: How does the example of the 'computer' explain the evolution of words?
column2: The meaning of a word can change over time even if the word remains the same.

column1: In a democracy, how should major decisions be handled after elections?
column2: Every major decision must go through a series of consultations.

column1: To whom are office bearers in a democracy accountable, besides the people?
column2: Other independent officials

column1: What must a democratic government respect regarding minorities?
column2: Guarantees to the minorities

column1: What is the core difference between a monarch's rule and an elected ruler's rule?
column2: Monarchs rule by birthright, while elected rulers are chosen by the people.

column1: Give an example from the text of a country where the army rules without election.
column2: Myanmar

column1: According to the text, what is the 'bare minimum' objective of defining democracy?
column2: To distinguish a democratic form of government from a non-democratic government.

column1: In the debate on democracy, what is the danger of a 'strong' leader who has all the powers?
column2: The person might start using those powers for themselves or their family (dictatorship).

column1: How does 'rule of law' protect citizens in a democracy?
column2: It ensures that everyone is equal in the eyes of the law.

column1: Why are elections in Mexico under the PRI party considered unfair?
column2: The PRI used dirty tricks and government resources to ensure they could never be defeated.

column1: In the summary definition, who takes all the major decisions in a democracy?
column2: Rulers elected by the people

column1: How does democracy address the 'human inclination to injustice'?
column2: By making democracy a necessary system to provide checks on power.

column1: Identify the non-democratic leader of Chile mentioned in the text.
column2: Pinochet

column1: What happened to Robert Mugabe in 2017?
column2: He was forced out of office.

column1: Why is the term 'one person, one vote, one value' significant?
column2: It emphasizes political equality where every individual's choice carries the same weight.

column1: What is the primary factor common to all democracies mentioned at the start of the chapter?
column2: The government is chosen by the people.
